origin.name: Cuba
origin.key_document: Certificado de defuncion (death certificate from Registro del Estado Civil)
origin.source: FCDO Cuba travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Havana guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Cuba is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certificado de defuncion (certified English translation required)
special_rules: Cuban bureaucracy can be slow; all communications and documentation are in Spanish.
All Spanish-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Fiscalia (Prosecutor's Office) must authorise release for unnatural or suspicious deaths.
UK travel to Cuba is common for holidays; notify your travel insurer immediately.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Cuban documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Havana can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Cuba to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Cuba. Repatriation to the UK takes 3-5 weeks. Spanish documentation, Fiscalia clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
